title: Nephrodium rubiginosum
taxonomy: Nephrodium rubiginosum belongs to the family Matoniaceae and is a species of fern. It is classified under the phylum Pteridophyta and the division Pteridophyta. The genus Nephrodium is now considered to be a synonym of the genus Matonia. Nephrodium rubiginosum is often classified as Matonia rubiginosa in modern taxonomy. This classification reflects the evolving understanding of fern classification and the relationships between different plant species.

intro: Nephrodium rubiginosum, also known as maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium rubiginosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
